name: Kourambiedes
recipeInstructions:
1. In an electric mixing bowl, whip the sweet butter until it is fluffy and white.
2. Gradually add the powdered sugar, egg yolk, and flavorings to the butter while continuing to beat on medium speed.
3. Sift the baking powder with the flour and gradually add it to the batter by hand. Work the flour thoroughly into the mixture before adding more.
4. Mix in the ground almonds.
5. Knead until a soft, buttery dough is formed that holds together when a small amount is rolled in your hands.
6. Break off pieces slightly larger than a walnut and roll them into balls, half-moons, or S-curves.
7. Place the shapes on cookie sheets with at least an inch of space between each one.
8. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes on the center rack of a preheated 350-degree oven, or until golden but not chestnut-colored.
9. Remove from the oven and carefully lift each kourambie. Place it on a generous layer of sifted powdered sugar.
10. Immediately sift more powdered sugar over the kourambiedes to cover them evenly.
11. Allow to cool for 10 to 15 minutes before lifting and rolling if necessary to ensure even coating. They may be served in individual fluted paper cups.
